operating_state: Total revenue increased 25% to $8.5 billion driven by semiconductor solutions growth from strong networking, server storage, and broadband demand plus higher wireless content in mobile devices. Infrastructure software grew 5% primarily from mainframe and FC SAN products. Apple represents approximately 20% of revenue with top five customers accounting for 35% of total revenue. Manufacturing operates on outsourced model with distributors accounting for 56% of net revenue.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 67% from 62% primarily driven by lower amortization of acquisition-related intangible assets from prior acquisitions and favorable semiconductor solutions margins. Operating expenses decreased as percentage of revenue due to lower CA Inc. intangible amortization and reduced restructuring costs, partially offset by higher variable compensation and research and development investments.
material_events: Entered VMware merger agreement in May 2022 for $61 billion cash-and-stock transaction expected to close in fiscal 2023 with $32 billion committed bridge financing secured. Thomas Krause resigned as President of Broadcom Software Group with CEO Hock Tan assuming responsibilities while Charlie Kawwas was named President of Semiconductor Solutions Group. Repurchased $7.0 billion of common stock and paid $5.3 billion in dividends during nine-month period.
macro_exposures: Supply chain constraints continue with wafer and substrate availability issues though lead times have stabilized. TSMC dependency remains significant at 90% of wafer manufacturing through contract manufacturers. Trade tensions between US and China create ongoing tariff and market access risks with export restrictions limiting sales to customers including Huawei requiring Department of Commerce licenses.
forward_outlook: VMware merger expected to complete in fiscal 2023 subject to regulatory approvals and stockholder adoption with integration planning continuing. Additional interest expense anticipated from VMware-related indebtedness. Capital allocation priorities remain operational spending, research and development investments, potential acquisitions, debt servicing, and returning cash through dividends and share repurchases.
uncertainty_profile: VMware merger faces regulatory clearance requirements across multiple jurisdictions with $1.5 billion termination fees for either party under specified circumstances. Substantial debt burden of $41.2 billion creates refinancing risk and limits financial flexibility. Tax incentives in Singapore expire 2025 and Malaysia 2028 potentially increasing effective tax rates while new U.S. book minimum tax and stock repurchase excise tax create additional cost pressures.
